summary: Matched additional code in main/melee/lb/lb_0192, especially reset/disc-status handling and a raw-pad/snapshot helper. The PR added localized GameCube disc error/read messages, implemented lb_800192A8 to display and update those messages during DVD drive error states, matched lb_8001955C and fn_800195FC, and moved lb_0192-owned prototypes out of lbcardnew.h into lb_0192.h. The decomp.dev bot reported 2 new 100% matches, lb_8001955C (+116 bytes) and fn_800195FC (+44 bytes), plus lb_800192A8 improved to 86.71% (+599 bytes).
what_changed: Added static localized message tables in lb_0192.c: jp_msg[6] and us_msg[6], each with three string slots for DVD cover, insert disc, wrong disc, unreadable disc, fatal error, and reading-disc states.
Added static HSD_Text* lb_804D63D0 for the SIS text object used by the disc-status UI.
Kept lb_80019230 as a static DVDGetDriveStatus classifier and wrapped it in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, indicating inlining control was necessary for the match.

Implemented lb_800192A8(void (*cb)(void)) as a disc-status loop: suppresses audio, toggles rumble, creates an HSD text object, chooses JP or US layout offsets, populates three text lines, renders each frame, optionally invokes a callback, polls lb_80019230 until status clears, then restores audio/rumble, destroys the text object, and sets gmMainLib_8046B0F0.xC = 1.

Implemented lb_8001955C reset-switch handling: if HSD_PadGetResetSwitch() is active, it stops audio, waits around lb_8001B6F8(), clears VI callbacks, blacks the screen, flushes/waits for retrace, and calls OSResetSystem; otherwise it continues polling card/input-related helpers.
Added fn_800195FC, which calls HSD_PadRenewRawStatus(0), lb_8001C600(), and lbSnap_8001D2BC().

Expanded lb_0192.h with prototypes for lb_800192A8, fn_800195FC, lb_80019880, lb_80019894, lb_800198E0, lb_80019900, lb_80019A30, lb_80019A48, and lb_80019AAC, and renamed the include guard from GALE01_019230 to MELEE_LB_0192_H.
Removed lb_0192 function prototypes from lbcardnew.h, reducing cross-header leakage and placing declarations with their owning source module.
Updated dberror.c and gmmain.c to include lb_0192.h directly where those declarations are needed.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt data annotations around 0x803BA708-0x803BAA60, marking many Japanese strings as data:sjis, splitting lb_803BA870/lb_803BA890 and lb_803BAA44/lb_803BAA60, and adding lbl_803BAA44 as a string symbol.
smart_moves: Used explicit JP and US message tables with a shared three-field struct, mirroring the data layout suggested by contiguous string/data symbols and simplifying language selection in lb_800192A8.
Controlled lb_80019230 with #pragma dont_inline, a targeted matching tactic that preserves call structure instead of relying on compiler heuristics.

Moved function declarations from lbcardnew.h to lb_0192.h and added direct includes in dberror.c and gmmain.c, which makes module ownership clearer and prevents unrelated card headers from carrying lb_0192 API surface.
Updated symbols.txt to identify Shift-JIS strings and split previously coarse data regions, aligning symbol metadata with the newly expressed localized string arrays.
Matched small wrapper/control functions first (lb_8001955C and fn_800195FC) while also making measurable progress on the larger lb_800192A8 function.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling message-display code, check symbols.txt for data:string versus data:sjis boundaries; correct string encoding and splitting can reveal array shapes and struct field counts.

Disc error UI code may combine multiple systems in one loop: DVD status polling, audio suppression/restoration, rumble enable/disable, SIS text creation/update, cache invalidation, render kickoff, XFB copy, and optional frame callbacks.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: declarations should live in the header for the source module that owns the functions, and users should include that header directly.
For matching small status-classifier helpers, explicitly preventing inlining can be necessary to keep the generated code aligned with the original binary.

Language-dependent layout constants can be evidence for separate JP/US branches rather than data-only localization; here JP and US use different HSD_Text x/y scaling/position values before drawing the same three-line message shape.
Reset-path matches can require preserving hardware sequencing: clear VI callbacks, set black, flush, wait two retraces, then OSResetSystem.
naming_conventions: Header guards for module headers use descriptive project/module style such as MELEE_LB_0192_H rather than address-derived GALE01_019230.
Address-based names remain for unresolved functions and data, e.g. lb_800192A8, lb_8001955C, lb_804D63D0, and lb_804329F0.
A generated/function-pointer style symbol name fn_800195FC was introduced for the newly matched helper at 0x800195FC.
Localized message arrays were named by language/content role: jp_msg and us_msg.
Anonymous or partially understood data structures retained placeholder field names such as x0, x4, x8 and lb_804329F0_t::UnkArrElem, reflecting limited semantic certainty.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on was applied specifically around static enum_t lb_80019230(void), then restored with #pragma pop.
PAD_STACK(8) was inserted in lb_800192A8, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
The implementation preserved float constants and text-line y offsets exactly in the rendering setup, including JP layout 0.67F/0.75F, US layout 0.58F/0.70F, and line offsets -68.0F, -34.0F, and 0.0F.
The render loop keeps the status-change guard using prev_i so text strings are only updated when the DVD status classifier changes.
The reset path preserves the do-while wait on lb_8001B6F8() == 0xB and the two VIWaitForRetrace calls before OSResetSystem.
Symbol metadata changes split data regions to better match compiler-emitted string/table boundaries, including sjis annotations for Japanese text.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code stayed at 33.50% overall but added +160 matched bytes in GALE01.
Two new functions reached 100%: lb_8001955C (+116 bytes) and fn_800195FC (+44 bytes).
review_feedback.evidence: Bot report lists both functions under '2 new matches'.

review_feedback.feedback: lb_800192A8 improved from 0.00% to 86.71%, adding +599 bytes of matched progress but remained unmatched.
review_feedback.evidence: Bot report lists lb_800192A8 under '1 improvements in unmatched functions'.

searchable_terms: Game Disc could not be read
Insert the Super Smash Bros. Melee Game Disc
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching lb_800192A8 after its 86.71% state in PR 1665.
Search for additional lb_0192.h cleanup or renaming of fn_800195FC to a semantic name.
Look for PRs that replace lb_804D63D0, jp_msg, us_msg, or struct strings with more descriptive names.
Search symbols.txt history around lb_803BA708 through lb_803BAA60 for further data:sjis/string boundary corrections.
Find other uses of #pragma dont_inline and PAD_STACK in lb or DVD/error UI matching work.
